title: Make every remote operation idempotent
description: Attach a dedup key to each request so the unavoidable retries from timeouts do not apply an operation twice.
body: Use when a client or peer can time out and retry a request, which is always in an asynchronous network. Do not assume a timeout means the operation did not happen, and avoid 'exactly-once' delivery promises that the network cannot keep. Actions: give every mutating request a stable idempotency key, record applied keys on the server, and return the prior result on a duplicate instead of re-applying. Validation signals include a duplicate-delivery test where the effect occurs exactly once and a log showing deduped retries. Risk boundary: non-idempotent retries double-charge, double-ship, or double-increment; treat any retried mutation without a dedup key as a correctness bug requiring fix before release.
